Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-06-2020 Continued To Aug-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Barteau, Esq., counsel for successor-in-interest Clarissa Perez; Trisha Friedeberg, Esq., counsel for personal representative David Keeler; Donald Sweet, Esq., counsel for Angelica Marquez, guardian ad litem for the minor beneficiary Julissa Cabrera. All counsel appear by telephone.

At the first calendar call, Mr. Sweet is not present and the Court passes the matter.

At the second call, all counsel are present. Court and counsel commence the hearing with discussion concerning the terms of the settlement agreement, the terms of the minor's compromise [with respect to Julissa Cabrera] and the scope of the hearing thereon, and counsel's overall fees and costs, including the will contest and the fees and costs related to the late-filed probate of the Bertha Cordova estate [mother of the decedent]. Upon inquiry by the Court, counsel offer estimates of the total fees and costs and what the estimated net distribution to the minor would be. Given the amorphous nature of the various estimates and discrepancies in the cost calculations, the Court is disinclined to approve the settlement at this time and requests more definitive information from counsel with respect to the attorney fees, costs, and the final net distribution to the minor.

After further discussion, the Court extends the letters of special administration of Mr. Keeler, but expands the scope of the administrator's authority with general powers. Ms. Friedeberg is to prepare the Order.

The Court continues all matters to August 10, 2020 with the expectation that counsel will file supplemental pleadings with a more definitive explanation of fees, costs, and net distribution amounts for the minor.

Judge: Hon. Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputies Choi and Nuque; Reporter: Not Reported. (D306)

1. The Petitioner's forensic expert is allowed to examine the original document, at a time and place agreed upon by the Clerk's Office.

3. A description of each and every non-destructive examination procedure the Petitioner's forensic expert will conduct on the document at the examination.
4. If the Petitioner wants a copy of the original document, the Order should provide for that allowance.

The ex parte petition was taken under submission, pending submission of a proposed order by Petitioner.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roxanne Jen for and with Nina Rossi (Petitioner).
Lindsey Munyer for and with Kathie Cordova (Mother of Decedent).
Donald Sweet for and with Angelica Cabrera Marquez (Guardian Ad Litem for Julissa Cabrera).

Ms. Munyer stated that her firm was just retained last week, that her Client will be filing a petition for probate within two weeks, that she reached out to Counsel to see if they could agree to appointment of a neutral third party and that they were unable to come to an agreement. Mr. Sweet stated that he believes validity of the will comes at issue, that if the will is invalid, the Guardian Ad Litem would have priorty, that Nina is a long time Friend, spoke of having an expedited trial on validity of the will and stated that he believes the lodged will should not be linked. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to July 31, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
